title: GetItem
text: GetItem Parses a list of items consisting of Name=Value pairs. int GetItem(unsigned char * List , unsigned char ** Name , int * NameLen , unsigned char ** Value , unsigned char ** Next ) Parameters Type Name Direction Description unsigned char * List IN List to build unsigned char * Name INOUT Name of item int * NameLen OUT Length of Name unsigned char ** Value OUT Value of item unsigned char ** Next OUT Pointer to next location; * Next should be zero on the first call. The caller should not change the value. Return Values Value Description 0 Value not found >0 Length of list Description This function parses a list of items in which items are separated by a byte with the value 27, 0x1b. Items are pairs in the form Name=Value . Given the name and value, the function returns a pointer to the value and its length. The function can be used to walk through a list if the name is NULL. If *next is NULL, it starts at the specified list ; otherwise it starts at the next unused item. The caller should save *next or the start of the list, as this is the current name for which namelen is returned. The list names and values may be in UTF-8. Separators such as = and space are in ASCII. In the following example, GetItem is being used to retrieve information from a list of parameters: Explain this code DrvArea->User = DrvArea->Buffer + total; len = GetItem(parameters, (USTRING*)"USER", &namelen, &value, &next); ustrncpy (DrvArea->User, value, len); total += len; DrvArea->Buffer[total++] = CHAR_EOS; Copy Related Links PutItem Builds a list of Name=Value pairs.
